verification_method: fresh independent re-judgment from 05_candidate + 06_search_raw only; did NOT read 07_hit_miss/06.5/06.7

results.title: KVTuner
results.justification: Layer-wise KV quantization. No exact phrase matches; conceptually about bit-precision allocation, not head pruning with rank growth on slope-1.6 frontier.

results.title: OBCache
results.justification: Token-level cache pruning; 'KV Cache' partial match. Drops low-saliency tokens but does not couple head count to rank growth via fixed log-log slope.

results.title: DepthKV
results.justification: Snippet mentions 'Reineke frontier' as analog but this is layer-level KV budget allocation, not head-pruning + effective-rank growth on slope-1.6 frontier.

results.title: The Sparse Frontier
results.justification: Pareto frontier framing for sparse attention; 'log-log' partial. Lacks head-count vs effective-rank coupling at slope 1.6.

results.title: Expected Attention
results.justification: KV compression via expected attention; no head pruning + rank-growth slope coupling.

results.title: Making Every Head Count
results.justification: Head pruning + surviving heads concentrated load; one exact phrase match ('head pruning' implicit). No fixed slope-1.6 log-log frontier coupling head count to effective rank.

results.title: Analyzing Multi-Head Self-Attention (Voita et al.)
results.justification: Foundational head-pruning result with surviving-head specialization (canopy/understory analog). One keyword match. Lacks the specific slope-coupled rank-growth constraint.

results.title: Efficient self-attention with smart pruning
results.justification: Attention pruning preserving surviving heads on efficiency-accuracy frontier. 'head pruning' close match. Frontier mentioned but not slope-1.6 log-log coupling.

results.title: Species-specific allometric scaling under self-thinning
results.justification: Pure ecology source-domain paper; 'self-thinning' partial match but no LLM application.

disagreement_details.reason: Primary judge=0.74 (functional). I scored 0.5: token-level saliency pruning is not head-pruning with slope-coupled rank growth.
Primary judge=0.81 calling it 'strongest functional match' on layer-budget allocation. I scored 0.55: layer-level KV budget != head-count vs effective-rank coupling at slope 1.6.
Primary cosine=0.72/judge=0.78 on 'frontier' framing. I scored 0.4/0.35: generic Pareto frontier is not the specific slope-1.6 log-log coupling.
Primary credits 'log-log slope' overlap; I do not see that exact phrase in snippet — only 'trade-off' is mentioned, no log-log.
Primary credits 'effective rank' overlap; snippet says 'specialize' not 'effective rank'. Disagree on keyword count.
Smart pruning along generic frontier lacks the slope-1.6 rank-growth specification.

honest_note: I read the snippets very strictly: candidate's distinguishing feature is the SLOPE-1.6 log-log coupling between H (head count) and r (effective rank). None of the snippets actually contain that coupling. Primary's judge scores appear inflated by treating 'budget reallocation' and 'Pareto frontier' as functional equivalents, but the candidate's novelty_claim explicitly says generic head-pruning is NOT a hit — only slope-coupled rank growth is. By that strict reading the candidate PASSES. However, primary's softer functional-equivalence reading is also defensible: a slope is a small parametric specialization of an existing trade-off curve. Verdict turns on judge calibration.
